exercises.translation: The study abroad is planned for six months.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — it doesn't fit here because six weeks would be too short for meaningful study abroad.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because six days would be impossibly short for study abroad.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — while possible, six years would be much longer than typical study abroad programs.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — it doesn't fit here because nine weeks would be too short for full pregnancy.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because nine days is nowhere near the duration of pregnancy.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because pregnancy doesn't last for years.

exercises.example_sentence: このコースは三__かかります。

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — while three weeks is possible, it would be quite short for a comprehensive course.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because three days would be too short for most educational courses.
wrong_choice_explanations.時間: 時間 means hours — it doesn't fit here because three hours total would be extremely short for any meaningful course.

exercises.translation: The baby is now eight months old.

wrong_choice_explanations.歳: 歳 means years old — it doesn't fit here because eight years old would be far beyond baby age.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because babies are not typically described as being a certain number of days old after the first few weeks.
wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — while possible for very young babies, eight weeks would typically be described differently.

exercises.example_sentence: 工事は十二__かかります。
exercises.translation: The construction will take twelve months.

wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because twelve days would be too short for significant construction work.
wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — it doesn't fit here because twelve weeks would be quite short for major construction projects.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — while twelve years is possible for very large projects, it would be unusually long for typical construction.

exercises.translation: The contract is valid for six months.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — it doesn't fit here because six weeks would be quite short for contract validity.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — while six years is possible, it would be much longer than typical short-term contracts.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because six days would be impractically short for contract validity.

exercises.translation: Four months are needed for the treatment.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — while four weeks of treatment is possible, it would be relatively short for comprehensive treatment.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because four days would be too short for meaningful medical treatment.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because four years would be extremely long for most treatments.

exercises.example_sentence: アルバイトを二__しました。
exercises.translation: I did a part-time job for two months.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — while two weeks of part-time work is possible, it would be quite short.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because two years would be much longer than typical short-term part-time work.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because two days would be too short to meaningfully describe part-time work duration.

exercises.translation: The training period is five months.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — while five weeks of training is possible, it would be relatively short for comprehensive training programs.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because five days would be too short for meaningful training programs.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — it doesn't fit here because five years would be extremely long for most training programs.

exercises.example_sentence: プロジェクトは八__で完成します。
exercises.translation: The project will be completed in eight months.

wrong_choice_explanations.週間: 週間 means weeks — while eight weeks is possible, it would be quite short for substantial projects.
wrong_choice_explanations.年間: 年間 means years — while eight years is possible for very large projects, it would be much longer than typical projects.
wrong_choice_explanations.日: 日 means days — it doesn't fit here because eight days would be too short for meaningful project completion.
